ministry: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
department: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
implementing_agency: MeitY Startup Hub through Software Technology Parks of India
target_beneficiaries: Designated incubation centres, technology entrepreneurs and ICT or emerging-technology startups supported through those centres.
startup_stage: Idea to proof of concept through Entrepreneur-in-Residence support; prototype development through grants; and product development or market outreach through investment support.
benefits: Official grant-release evidence records implementation funding for SASACT through STPI.
sector_evidence: Official scheme material establishes startup acceleration, incubation, innovation and funding support.
sector_reason: The scheme supports technology startups through acceleration, incubation and structured financial or market support.
startup_beneficiary_evidence: Designated incubation centres, technology entrepreneurs and ICT or emerging-technology startups supported through those centres.
startup_access_evidence: Historical TIDE 2.0 support was delivered through participating incubation centres. The approved period ended on 2024-01-31; no current central application route is asserted.

ministry: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
department: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
implementing_agency: MeitY Startup Hub through approximately 50 Implementing Agencies
target_beneficiaries: Eligible accelerators as direct scheme partners and selected product-based or IT-based startups as downstream beneficiaries.
startup_stage: Potential technology and software-product startups requiring acceleration from product or prototype development towards commercialization and growth.
eligibility: DPIIT-registered startup; at least 51% Indian promoter shareholding at application; additional component-specific selection requirements apply.
benefits: Support may include early-stage funding up to INR 10 lakh, pilot/investment support up to INR 50 lakh, and deep-tech support up to INR 1 crore, subject to component rules.
sector_evidence: Official scheme material establishes startup acceleration, incubation, innovation and funding support.
sector_reason: The scheme supports technology startups through acceleration, incubation and structured financial or market support.
startup_beneficiary_evidence: Eligible accelerators as direct scheme partners and selected product-based or IT-based startups as downstream beneficiaries.
startup_access_evidence: Support is delivered through selected accelerators or incubation centres; no currently open central application route is asserted.
